headline: Public pressure bloc is above the modeled 81 MP trigger. Pressure Index 59/100 (elevated). Polymarket's top Starmer-exit signal is 10.5%.

resignCalls.label: Calling for exit
resignCalls.note: MPs calling for Starmer to resign or set a timetable.

supporters.label: Backing Starmer
supporters.note: MPs reported signing a support statement backing the PM.

threshold.label: Contest threshold
threshold.note: 20% of current PLP (402) per UK Parliament Members API.

ministersOut.label: Ministers out
ministersOut.note: Ministerial resignations found in tracker/news copy.

pressure.note: 97 MPs are recorded as calling for an exit or timetable, 16 above the modeled contest threshold; the public support bloc is 14 larger than the pressure bloc. A public count is not the same as a formal leadership challenge.

pressureIndex.formula: Weighted: 35% PLP exit-call share (saturates at 1.5× the contest trigger so MPs above 81 still register), 15% support deficit, 10% minister exits, 25% Polymarket exit probability, 15% news intensity × negativity over a 12h window (negation-aware lexicon ensembled with winkNLP). Each input is normalised to 0-1 before weighting; missing inputs are dropped and remaining weights re-scaled.

components.label: Minister-exit momentum
components.raw: 5 exits · escalation boost ×1.5 paired senior resignations

components.label: News intensity × negativity
components.raw: 28 clusters (12h, 3-shingle Jaccard ≥0.5), 28 scored via ensemble (winkNLP + lexicon), avg 0.17

resignationCalls.name: Lee Pitcher
resignationCalls.constituency: Doncaster East and the Isle of Axholme

factions.name: Wes Streeting
factions.role: Former Health Secretary — out of cabinet
factions.posture: Out of cabinet. Resignation reframes him as the live succession candidate — every public move now reads as positioning. Watch for an explicit leadership bid, a campaign team, and any allied MPs declaring.
factions.latestMove: Streeting says raising concerns over Gaza in government like ‘hitting up against a brick wall’ - The Guardian
signals: The Guardian: Streeting says raising concerns over Gaza in government like ‘hitting up against a brick wall’ - The Guardian
Zubir Ahmed left a health role; treat this as a Streeting-orbit pressure signal, not proof of direction.

factions.name: Andy Burnham
factions.role: Greater Manchester mayor and external Labour alternative
factions.posture: External route pressure: his leverage is membership and PLP appetite, but any path still depends on parliamentary access or a mediated role.
factions.latestMove: Andy Burnham could introduce 10-year plan to nationalise ‘broken’ water industry
signals: Independent UK Politics: Andy Burnham could introduce 10-year plan to nationalise ‘broken’ water industry
Parsed source text says any transition should include giving Burnham a path into the conversation.

factions.name: Keir Starmer
factions.posture: Defence is built on public loyalty numbers, cabinet discipline, and making challengers show their hand.
factions.latestMove: 111 MPs reported on the support statement
signals: 111 MPs are reported backing the PM versus 97 calling for an exit or timetable.
Parsed source copy characterises Starmer as defiant in the face of resignation demands.
The dashboard separates public declarations from a formal internal trigger.

factions.name: Angela Rayner
factions.role: Deputy PM and continuity or unity option
factions.posture: Visible in market pricing and succession chatter, but less central to the current Streeting-Burnham pressure lane.
factions.latestMove: Track as a control signal against Streeting and Burnham
signals: Polymarket prices Rayner in the next-PM market.

proxyGroups.name: Soft Left / Tribune
proxyGroups.note: Soft-left tendency. Tribune Group of MPs, Compass-aligned. Membership is informal — treat as illustrative.

proxyGroups.name: Blue Labour
proxyGroups.note: Communitarian tendency around Maurice Glasman. Few formal MPs.

baselines.notes: Peak public pressure values for prior UK PMs at moment of acute crisis. Indicative only — drawn from press/Wikipedia summaries. PLP-trigger style metrics differ between Labour and Conservative parties.

events.label: Liz Truss (Oct 2022)
events.note: Resigned 20 Oct 2022 after 49 days. Cabinet revolt, market crisis, mass public withdrawal of support.

events.label: Boris Johnson (Jul 2022)
events.note: Cascade of 50+ government resignations forced exit on 7 Jul 2022. Earlier confidence vote Jun 2022 lost 211-148 (41% against).

events.label: Theresa May (Dec 2018 / May 2019)
events.note: Won 1922 confidence vote Dec 2018 (200-117, 37% against). Announced resignation 24 May 2019 after Brexit deadlock.

events.label: Jeremy Corbyn (Jun 2016)
events.note: PLP no-confidence vote 172-40 (81% against). Forced leadership contest; Corbyn re-elected by members.

events.label: Gordon Brown (Jun 2009)
events.note: James Purnell resignation triggered cabinet challenge; Brown survived after Mandelson/Darling held firm.

lines.text: Top story (1 source): Politics latest: Officer involved in Henry Nowak case resigns after Starmer says he ‘felt sick’ watching bodycam footage
Escalation stage 4/6 — Paired senior exits; paired senior resignations.
Biggest Polymarket move (1h): ▲ 61.0pp — Starmer out by December 31, 2026?

nextCatalysts.label: Escalation stage 4/6 — Paired senior exits
nextCatalysts.detail: Truss/Johnson timeline mapping. Stage 4+ corresponds to paired senior resignations; stage 5 is delegation/1922-style language; stage 6 is resignation imminent.

nextCatalysts.label: Paired senior resignations detected (5 cabinet/sec-of-state exits, 0 senior-resign headlines).
nextCatalysts.detail: Two senior departures within 48h is the Truss/Johnson cascade signal — within 24-48h of this pattern, both PMs were out.

nextCatalysts.label: Trigger threshold passed (97/81 MPs).
nextCatalysts.detail: 20% PLP rule has been met. Pressure now depends on cabinet stability and any formal challenger.

nextCatalysts.label: 5 ministerial exits recorded.
nextCatalysts.detail: Cabinet stability breaking. Watch for cascade — three or more exits historically precedes leader resignations.

nextCatalysts.label: No formal leadership-challenge language detected in headlines (72h).
nextCatalysts.detail: Keyword sweep for 'leadership bid', 'announces challenge', 'candidacy', etc.
